title: source lifecycle hooks (construct/destruct/entry/exit/rejection/any-transition/any-action)
manager: The full set of lifecycle hook points declarable in source — construct, destruct, entry, exit, rejection, any-transition, any-action — so a machine can react to every stage of its own life directly in FSL rather than only through host-side wiring. This makes lifecycle behavior part of the machine's definition, and it consolidates a long list of separately-requested hook grammars into one coherent, spelled-out set.
cs: construct/destruct/entry/exit/rejection/any-transition/any-action hooks in source (§12, fsl#1357); consolidates the on_* grammar cluster; registered through the uniform registry (W6.77).
